title: trisetum caudulatum
intro: Trisetum caudulatum, also known as the hairy oat grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, derived from its leaves and roots. The health benefits of Trisetum caudulatum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Trisetum caudulatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
